It is synced to agent-specific files (CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, etc.) via: ```powershell powershell -File scripts/sync-agent-context.ps1 ``` --- ## Project Overview A **monorepo** for a construction industry platform. Core capability: voice-enabled data capture for field workers, with AI extraction of structured data from audio and PDF documents. **Stack:** Hono API · SolidJS frontends · Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Realtime) · Python doc-parser --- ## Architecture ``` voicelog-scaffold/ ├── apps/ │ ├── api/ # Hono backend (The Brain) — port 3000 │ ├── web-dashboard/ # SolidJS admin — port 3001 │ ├── site-companion/ # SolidJS PWA (field workers) — port 3002 │ ├── material-admin/ # Material & taxonomy manager — port 3003 │ ├── work-orders/ # Work orders management — port 3004 │ └── tenant-portal/ # Tenant data portal — port 3005 ├── doc-parser/ # Python FastAPI AI service — port 5000 ├── packages/ │ ├── schemas/ # Zod schemas — THE source of truth │ ├── db/ # Auto-generated Supabase TypeScript types │ └── ui/ # Shared design system └── supabase/ # Migrations, seed, config ``` **Development workflow:** Schema-first. Define data in `packages/schemas` → API route → UI. --- ## ⛔ Critical Agent Rules — doc-parser **doc-parser is a standalone Docker container. It is NOT a local process inside the Agent Zero container.** | Rule | Detail | |------|--------| | ❌ NEVER run `server.py` directly | It runs inside its own container, not here | | ❌ NEVER install Python packages in `doc-parser/` | No pip, no venv, no requirements.txt installs from this container | | ❌ NEVER look for or activate a venv in `doc-parser/` | There is no venv there — it lives inside the Docker image | | ✅ If port 5000 is unreachable | `notify_user` type=warning and ask user to run `docker-compose up` in `doc-parser/` **on Windows** | | ✅ To interact with doc-parser | Use HTTP via `http://doc-parser:5000` (shared `agent-zero-network`) or `http://localhost:5000` | | ✅ To run CLI tools | Use `process_document.py` from this container pointing at the running API | > **Why this matters:** This mistake has caused repeated harm — installing packages, trying to start the server locally, and wasting session time. When the health check fails, the ONLY correct action is to notify the user to start the container on Windows. --- ## Port Assignments | Port | Service | |-------|-----------------| | 3000 | API (Hono) | | 3001 | Web Dashboard | | 3002 | Site Companion | | 3003 | Material Admin | | 3004 | Work Orders | | 3005 | Tenant Portal | | 5000 | Doc Parser | | 8082 | NocoDB | | 54321 | Supabase API | | 54323 | Supabase Studio | | 5080 | Agent Zero | --- ## Local Supabase Credentials > These are standard local Supabase dev credentials. Run `npx supabase status` to confirm. | Key | Value | |--------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | URL | `http://127.0.0.1:54321` | | Anon Key | `eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZS1kZW1vIiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJleHAiOjE5ODM4MTI5OTZ9.CRXP1A7WOeoJeXxjNni43kdQwgnWNReilDMblYTn_I0` | | Service Role | Run `npx supabase status` to get — needed for admin operations and doc-parser uploads | | Studio UI | `http://localhost:54323` | --- ## API Keys | Key | Location | |----------------|----------------------------| | OpenRouter | `doc-parser/.env` | --- ## Seed User Credentials | App | Email | Password | |----------------------------------|---------------------|-------------| | Web Dashboard (3001), Work Orders (3004) | `alice@example.com` | `password123` | | Tenant Portal (3005) | `eric@example.com` | `password123$` | > If Supabase is reset (`supabase db reset`), re-run `apps/api/scripts/create_user.ts` to recreate users. --- ## Environment Flags | Variable | Location | Effect | |------------------|---------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------| | `PROGRAM_STATUS=develop_without_ai` | root `.env`, `doc-parser/.env` | Skips Claude API calls in doc-parser; returns mocked JSON | | `REQUIRE_AUTH=true` | `apps/api/.env` | Enables JWT auth enforcement (disabled by default for local dev) | --- ## Doc-Parser Service **CRITICAL: Must run as Docker.** Do not run natively — requires system C++ deps (Tesseract, Poppler, OpenCV). ```bash cd doc-parser docker compose up -d --build ``` ### Endpoints | Method | Path | Description | |--------|--------------------|----------------------------------------| | POST | `/parse/docling` | Docling-only engine (primary) | | POST | `/parse/hybrid` | Hybrid: Docling decompose + Claude extract | | GET | `/status/{job_id}` | Poll for progress (safe to call every 2-5s) | | GET | `/results/{job_id}`| Fetch full results once complete | | GET | `/health` | Dependency status check | | GET | `/jobs` | List all jobs | ### Hono API — doc-parser routes (`/api/doc-parser/*`) The Hono API (port 3000) wraps the doc-parser with Supabase integration. All routes accept/return JSON. `document_id` must exist in `project_documents`. #### Pipeline endpoints (blocking — submit + poll + persist to DB) | Method | Path | Description | |--------|------|-------------| | `POST` | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline` | **Full pipeline** — parse entire PDF, persist all pages + OCR to DB. Blocks until complete. | | `POST` | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline/page` | **Single-page pipeline** — extract page N via `pdf-lib`, parse, upsert that page only. Body: `{ document_id, page_number, engine? }` | | `POST` | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline/reprocess` | **Idempotent re-run** — same as `/pipeline` but safe to call multiple times (upsert). Adds `reprocessed: true` in response. | #### Job control endpoints (async — caller manages polling) | Method | Path | Description | |--------|------|-------------| | `POST` | `/api/doc-parser/parse` | Submit job, return `job_id` immediately (caller polls) | | `GET` | `/api/doc-parser/status/:jobId` | Lightweight poll — safe every 2-5s | | `GET` | `/api/doc-parser/results/:jobId` | Full results — fetch once after `completed` | | `GET` | `/api/doc-parser/health` | Health check including doc-parser reachability | #### Read endpoints (DB queries — no doc-parser call) | Method | Path | Description | |--------|------|-------------| | `GET` | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/pages` | List all parsed pages. Query params: `?category=floorplan\|table\|site_photo\|diagram\|mixed\|text`, `?limit=`, `?offset=` | | `GET` | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/pages/:pageNumber` | Get one page by 1-indexed page number (storage_url, thumbnail, category, raw_text, metadata) | | `GET` | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/ocr` | List OCR/table results. Query params: `?page_number=N`, `?limit=`, `?offset=` | | `GET` | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/ocr/:pageNumber` | OCR results for a single page — returns page metadata + all OCR/table rows for that page | #### Document manager endpoints (company/project scope) | Method | Path | Description | |--------|------|-------------| | `GET` | `/api/doc-parser/projects/:projectId/documents` | List all documents for a project — id, title, description, file_url, page_count, ocr_table_count, parsed status. Query: `?limit=`, `?offset=` | | `GET` | `/api/doc-parser/projects/:projectId/documents/:documentId` | Full document detail — metadata + page list with thumbnails + category breakdown + OCR table count | > **Note:** `/pipeline/page` requires `pdf-lib`. Run `pnpm install` on Windows after any container-side `package.json` change. #### Pipeline endpoint — quick reference ```bash curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"document_id": "", "engine": "hybrid"}' # Returns: { document_id, job_id, pages_persisted, ocr_tables_persisted, summary } ``` > Full docs: `.agents/workflows/parse-document.md` ### Pipeline (Docling engine) 1. **Docling** — extracts text, tables, images from PDF 2. **Classification** — Tesseract + color variance separates floorplans / site photos / diagrams 3. **LLM analysis** — Claude 3.5 Sonnet (via OpenRouter) extracts rooms, doors, windows, areas 4. **Cross-page synthesis** — second LLM call consolidates multi-page results 5. **Confidence scoring** — quality score attached to result ### LLM Provider Default: `anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet` via OpenRouter (LiteLLM under the hood). Override with `DEFAULT_LLM_PROVIDER` env var. --- ## Starting the Stack ```powershell # Full reset + restart (kills zombies, resets DB, seeds, starts dev servers) powershell -File scripts/reset-and-start.ps1 # Or manually: npx supabase start pnpm dev # Doc-parser (separate): cd doc-parser && docker compose up -d ``` ```powershell # Kill zombie Node processes on ports 3000-3005 powershell -File scripts/kill-dev-servers.ps1 # Nuclear option taskkill /F /IM node.exe ``` --- ## Supabase Troubleshooting - **Realtime not updating**: Check RLS policies and that the table is in `supabase_realtime` publication. - **`supabase start` parse errors**: Comment out `deno_version` / `health_timeout` in `supabase/config.toml`. - **`major_version`**: Must be `17`. Do not downgrade. --- ## Key Docs | File | Topic | |------|-------| | `docs/ports.md` | Port allocation contract | | `docs/testing.md` | Test credentials and agent guidelines | | `docs/understanding_architecture.md` | Architecture deep dive | | `docs/a2a/README.md` | A2A (Agent-to-Agent) JSON-RPC protocol | | `docs/development_best_practices.md` | Dev conventions | (venv) root@d2d162d983f0:/a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app#", "file": "/a0/usr/chats/ZXw6Gr7v/messages/10.txt"}, "summary": "", "tokens": 3213}, {"_cls": "Message", "ai": true, "content": " Now I have the full picture from AGENTS.md. footer_sample: any; general_notes: string[]; synthesis: any; confidence: DocParserConfidence; summary: DocParserResultSummary; } /** * Submit a PDF for parsing with the specified engine. */ export async function submitParseJob( pdfBuffer: Buffer | ArrayBuffer, fileName: string, engine: 'docling' | 'hybrid', options: { provider?: string; tenantName?: string; projectName?: string; supabaseUrl?: string; supabaseKey?: string; } = {}, ): Promise { const endpoint = engine === 'hybrid' ? '/parse/hybrid' : '/parse/docling'; const formData = new FormData(); const blob = new Blob([new Uint8Array(pdfBuffer as any)], { type: 'application/pdf' }); formData.append('file', blob, fileName); if (options.provider) { formData.append('provider', options.provider); } if (options.tenantName) { formData.append('tenant_name', options.tenantName); } if (options.projectName) { formData.append('project_name', options.projectName); } if (options.supabaseUrl) { formData.append('supabase_url', options.supabaseUrl); } if (options.supabaseKey) { formData.append('supabase_key', options.supabaseKey); } const response = await fetch(`${DOC_PARSER_URL}${endpoint}`, { method: 'POST', body: formData, }); if (!response.ok) { const errorText = await response.text(); throw new Error( `Doc-parser submission failed (${response.status}): ${errorText}`, ); } return response.json(); } /** * Get lightweight job status (poll-safe). * NEVER includes result data. */ export async function getJobStatus( jobId: string, ): Promise { const response = await fetch(`${DOC_PARSER_URL}/status/${jobId}`); if (!response.ok) { if (response.status === 404) { throw new Error(`Job ${jobId} not found`); } throw new Error(`Status check failed (${response.status})`); } return response.json(); } /** * Get full extraction results (fetch-once after completed). * Heavy payload - only call when status confirms "completed". */ export async function getJobResults( jobId: string, summaryOnly: boolean = false, ): Promise<{ job_id: string; status: string; result?: DocParserResult; summary?: DocParserResultSummary }> { const url = summaryOnly ? If you are installing with npm, you can try not copying the "node_modules" directory when you copy the files over, and running "npm ci" or "npm install" on the destination platform after the copy. Or you could consider using yarn instead of npm which has built-in support for installing a package on multiple platforms simultaneously. If you are installing with yarn, you can try listing both this platform and the other platform in your ".yarnrc.yml" file using the "supportedArchitectures" feature: https://yarnpkg.com/configuration/yarnrc/#supportedArchitectures Keep in mind that this means multiple copies of esbuild will be present. logs.content: **Hello! 👋**, I'm **Agent Zero**, your AI assistant. How can I help you today?

## Session Goal: Parse test PDF to disk + fix image URL bug in doc-parser pipeline

Read these files first (in order):
1. /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/AGENTS.md
2. /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/.agents/workflows/parse-document.md
3. /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/doc-parser/server.py
4. /a0/usr/projects/timesheet_app/voicelog-scaffold/apps/api/src/routes/doc-parser-routes.ts

---

### Task 1 — Parse test PDF and save individual pages to disk

The test PDF is at:
  tests/test_seed/Sch'eyk DRAWINGS BH 10 08 2024rs_260113_165135.pdf

Parsing is very resource-intensive. Parse this PDF ONCE via the doc-parser API
and save all results to a local folder so future testing can reuse the output
without re-parsing.

Save to: `doc-parser/tests/artifacts/runs/`

For each page, save:
- Full page render image (PNG)
- Thumbnail image (PNG)
- Raw text (TXT)
- Full page metadata (JSON)
- Any detected tables as markdown (MD)
- Any detected sub-elements (floorplans, photos, diagrams) as cropped images

Also save:
- The complete raw API response as `full_result.json`
- A manifest file `manifest.json` listing all saved files with page numbers

Use the async job API directly (not the Hono pipeline):
  POST http://doc-parser:5000/parse/hybrid  (submit)
  GET  http://doc-parser:5000/status/<job_id>  (poll)
  GET  http://doc-parser:5000/results/<job_id>  (fetch)

Write a Python script using `requests` to do this from the Agent Zero container.
Save the script as `doc-parser/tests/save_parsed_pages.py` for reuse.

---

### Task 2 — Fix the image URL field name mismatch (KNOWN BUG)

There is a confirmed field name mismatch between doc-parser and the Hono pipeline.

**doc-parser server.py returns per page:**
```python
pages_result[page_no]["page_image_url"] = page_url      # line 418
pages_result[page_no]["thumbnail_url"]  = thumb_url      # line 426

content_copy
Hono doc-parser-routes.ts reads per page:

storage_url:   pd?.storage_url ?? pd?.image_url ?? null,   // line 181
thumbnail_url: pd?.thumbnail_url ?? null,                   // line 182

content_copy
The bug: page_image_url does NOT match storage_url or image_url.
Result: thumbnail_url persists correctly, but storage_url is always NULL.

Fix approach — update the Hono fallback chain (preferred):
Change line 181 (and the identical line 562) to include page_image_url:

storage_url: pd?.storage_url ?? pd?.page_image_url ?? pd?.image_url ?? null,

content_copy
Do NOT change server.py — other consumers may depend on page_image_url.

After fixing, verify by inspecting the saved full_result.json from Task 1:

Confirm each page object has page_image_url (not storage_url)
Confirm the Hono mapping would now correctly pick it up
Task 3 — Verify with saved artifacts (no re-parsing needed)
Using the files saved in Task 1, write a verification script that:

Loads full_result.json
For each page, checks that page_image_url exists and is a valid Supabase URL
For each page, checks that thumbnail_url exists and is a valid Supabase URL
Simulates the Hono mapping logic (both old and fixed) to show the difference
Prints a summary table:
| Page | page_image_url | thumbnail_url | old_mapping | fixed_mapping |
Save as: doc-parser/tests/verify_image_urls.py

Environment rules (CRITICAL)
Doc-parser runs in its own Docker container on port 5000
NEVER run server.py directly or install Python packages in doc-parser/
If port 5000 is unreachable → notify_user and ask to start it on Windows
Agent Zero container reaches doc-parser via http://doc-parser:5000
All scripts run inside the Agent Zero container using requests library
Prerequisites
Doc-parser container running on Windows: cd doc-parser && docker-compose up
Supabase running on Windows: supabase start
Paste supabase status output so I have the service role key

---

This prompt gives the new session everything it needs: the exact bug with line numbers, a clear three-task sequence, and the constraint to save parsed output to disk so you never need to re-parse during testing.

# doc-parser Hono API — Complete Route Table (Updated 2026-03-12)

All routes under `/api/doc-parser/*` in `apps/api/src/routes/doc-parser-routes.ts` (1028 lines).
Documentation: `.agents/workflows/parse-document.md` (6 approaches, 293 lines).
AGENTS.md is the single source of truth — sync with `powershell -File scripts/sync-agent-context.ps1`.

## Pipeline endpoints (blocking — submit + poll + persist to DB)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline` | Full pipeline — parse entire PDF, persist all pages + OCR. Blocks until complete. |
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline/page` | Single-page — extract page N via `pdf-lib`, parse, upsert that page only. Body: `{ document_id, page_number, engine? }` |
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline/reprocess` | Idempotent re-run — same as /pipeline but safe to call multiple times (upsert). Returns `reprocessed: true`. |

## Job control endpoints (async — caller manages polling)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/parse` | Submit job, return job_id immediately |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/status/:jobId` | Lightweight poll — safe every 2-5s |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/results/:jobId` | Full results — fetch once after completed |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/health` | Health check including doc-parser reachability |

## Read endpoints (DB queries — no doc-parser call)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/pages` | List all parsed pages. Query: `?category=floorplan|table|site_photo|diagram|mixed|text`, `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/pages/:pageNumber` | Get one page by 1-indexed number (storage_url, thumbnail, category, raw_text, metadata) |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/ocr` | List OCR/table results. Query: `?page_number=N`, `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/ocr/:pageNumber` | OCR for a single page — returns page metadata + all OCR/table rows for that page |

## Document manager endpoints (project/company scope)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/projects/:projectId/documents` | List all documents for a project — id, title, description, file_url, page_count, ocr_table_count, parsed status. Query: `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/projects/:projectId/documents/:documentId` | Full document detail — metadata + page list with thumbnails + category breakdown + OCR table count |

## Schema
- `project_documents.project_id` -> `wo_projects.id` -> `organizations.id`
- `document_pages.document_id` -> `project_documents.id`
- `ocr_results.document_id` -> `project_documents.id`, `ocr_results.page_id` -> `document_pages.id`

## Notes
- `document_id` must exist in `project_documents` table
- Pipeline endpoints use `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` (bypasses RLS)
- `/pipeline/page` requires `pdf-lib` — run `pnpm install` on Windows after package.json change
- DB tables: `document_pages`, `ocr_results` (migration: `20260312000001_document_pages_ocr.sql`)
- Scoping hierarchy: organizations -> wo_projects -> project_documents -> document_pages -> ocr_results

Test artifacts and parsed results should be saved to 'doc-parser/tests/artifacts/runs/' to avoid resource-intensive re-parsing during testing.

The Hono API 'POST /api/doc-parser/pipeline' endpoint automates the full PDF processing workflow: downloading from Supabase, parsing via doc-parser, and persisting results to 'document_pages' and 'ocr_results' tables.

The test PDF 'Sch'eyk DRAWINGS BH 10 08 2024rs_260113_165135.pdf' is located at 'tests/test_seed/' and is symlinked to 'doc-parser/tests/artifacts/pdfs/scheyk_drawings.pdf' for testing purposes.

The 'pdf-lib' library is used in 'apps/api' for single-page PDF extraction. For page-specific operations, use the 'copyPages(srcDoc, indices)' method. Note: 'copyPagesFrom' is an incorrect method name and was corrected in 'doc-parser-routes.ts'.

# Construction Voice Platform — Agent Context

This file is the single source of truth for all AI agents working on this project.
It is synced to agent-specific files (CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, etc.) via:
```powershell
powershell -File scripts/sync-agent-context.ps1
```

---

## Project Overview

A **monorepo** for a construction industry platform. Core capability: voice-enabled data capture
for field workers, with AI extraction of structured data from audio and PDF documents.

**Stack:** Hono API · SolidJS frontends · Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Realtime) · Python doc-parser

---

## Architecture

```
voicelog-scaffold/
├── apps/
│   ├── api/              # Hono backend (The Brain) — port 3000
│   ├── web-dashboard/    # SolidJS admin — port 3001
│   ├── site-companion/   # SolidJS PWA (field workers) — port 3002
│   ├── material-admin/   # Material & taxonomy manager — port 3003
│   ├── work-orders/      # Work orders management — port 3004
│   └── tenant-portal/    # Tenant data portal — port 3005
├── doc-parser/           # Python FastAPI AI service — port 5000
├── packages/
│   ├── schemas/          # Zod schemas — THE source of truth
│   ├── db/               # Auto-generated Supabase TypeScript types
│   └── ui/               # Shared design system
└── supabase/             # Migrations, seed, config
```

**Development workflow:** Schema-first. Define data in `packages/schemas` → API route → UI.

---

## ⛔ Critical Agent Rules — doc-parser

**doc-parser is a standalone Docker container. It is NOT a local process inside the Agent Zero container.**

| Rule | Detail |
|------|--------|
| ❌ NEVER run `server.py` directly | It runs inside its own container, not here |
| ❌ NEVER install Python packages in `doc-parser/` | No pip, no venv, no requirements.txt installs from this container |
| ❌ NEVER look for or activate a venv in `doc-parser/` | There is no venv there — it lives inside the Docker image |
| ✅ If port 5000 is unreachable | `notify_user` type=warning and ask user to run `docker-compose up` in `doc-parser/` **on Windows** |
| ✅ To interact with doc-parser | Use HTTP via `http://doc-parser:5000` (shared `agent-zero-network`) or `http://localhost:5000` |
| ✅ To run CLI tools | Use `process_document.py` from this container pointing at the running API |

> **Why this matters:** This mistake has caused repeated harm — installing packages, trying to start the server locally, and wasting session time. When the health check fails, the ONLY correct action is to notify the user to start the container on Windows.

---

## Port Assignments

| Port  | Service         |
|-------|-----------------|
| 3000  | API (Hono)      |
| 3001  | Web Dashboard   |
| 3002  | Site Companion  |
| 3003  | Material Admin  |
| 3004  | Work Orders     |
| 3005  | Tenant Portal   |
| 5000  | Doc Parser      |
| 8082  | NocoDB          |
| 54321 | Supabase API    |
| 54323 | Supabase Studio |
| 5080  | Agent Zero      |

---

## Local Supabase Credentials

> These are standard local Supabase dev credentials. Run `npx supabase status` to confirm.

| Key          | Value                                                                                                                                                                    |
|--------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| URL          | `http://127.0.0.1:54321`                                                                                                                                                 |
| Anon Key     | `eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZS1kZW1vIiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJleHAiOjE5ODM4MTI5OTZ9.CRXP1A7WOeoJeXxjNni43kdQwgnWNReilDMblYTn_I0` |
| Service Role | Run `npx supabase status` to get �� needed for admin operations and doc-parser uploads                                                                                    |
| Studio UI    | `http://localhost:54323`                                                                                                                                                 |

---

## API Keys

| Key            | Location                   |
|----------------|----------------------------|
| OpenRouter     | `doc-parser/.env`          |

---

## Seed User Credentials

| App                              | Email               | Password    |
|----------------------------------|---------------------|-------------|
| Web Dashboard (3001), Work Orders (3004) | `alice@example.com` | `password123`  |
| Tenant Portal (3005)             | `eric@example.com`  | `password123$` |

> If Supabase is reset (`supabase db reset`), re-run `apps/api/scripts/create_user.ts` to recreate users.

---

## Environment Flags

| Variable         | Location            | Effect                                                          |
|------------------|---------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| `PROGRAM_STATUS=develop_without_ai` | root `.env`, `doc-parser/.env` | Skips Claude API calls in doc-parser; returns mocked JSON |
| `REQUIRE_AUTH=true` | `apps/api/.env`   | Enables JWT auth enforcement (disabled by default for local dev) |

---

## Doc-Parser Service

**CRITICAL: Must run as Docker.** Do not run natively — requires system C++ deps (Tesseract, Poppler, OpenCV).

```bash
cd doc-parser
docker compose up -d --build
```

### Endpoints

| Method | Path               | Description                            |
|--------|--------------------|----------------------------------------|
| POST   | `/parse/docling`   | Docling-only engine (primary)          |
| POST   | `/parse/hybrid`    | Hybrid: Docling decompose + Claude extract |
| GET    | `/status/{job_id}` | Poll for progress (safe to call every 2-5s) |
| GET    | `/results/{job_id}`| Fetch full results once complete       |
| GET    | `/health`          | Dependency status check                |
| GET    | `/jobs`            | List all jobs                          |

### Hono API — doc-parser routes (`/api/doc-parser/*`)

The Hono API (port 3000) wraps the doc-parser with Supabase integration.
All routes accept/return JSON. `document_id` must exist in `project_documents`.

#### Pipeline endpoints (blocking — submit + poll + persist to DB)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `POST` | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline` | **Full pipeline** — parse entire PDF, persist all pages + OCR to DB. Blocks until complete. |
| `POST` | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline/page` | **Single-page pipeline** — extract page N via `pdf-lib`, parse, upsert that page only. Body: `{ document_id, page_number, engine? }` |
| `POST` | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline/reprocess` | **Idempotent re-run** — same as `/pipeline` but safe to call multiple times (upsert). Adds `reprocessed: true` in response. |

#### Job control endpoints (async — caller manages polling)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `POST` | `/api/doc-parser/parse` | Submit job, return `job_id` immediately (caller polls) |
| `GET`  | `/api/doc-parser/status/:jobId` | Lightweight poll — safe every 2-5s |
| `GET`  | `/api/doc-parser/results/:jobId` | Full results — fetch once after `completed` |
| `GET`  | `/api/doc-parser/health` | Health check including doc-parser reachability |

#### Read endpoints (DB queries — no doc-parser call)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `GET`  | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/pages` | List all parsed pages. Query params: `?category=floorplan\|table\|site_photo\|diagram\|mixed\|text`, `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| `GET`  | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/pages/:pageNumber` | Get one page by 1-indexed page number (storage_url, thumbnail, category, raw_text, metadata) |
| `GET`  | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/ocr` | List OCR/table results. Query params: `?page_number=N`, `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| `GET`  | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/ocr/:pageNumber` | OCR results for a single page — returns page metadata + all OCR/table rows for that page |

#### Document manager endpoints (company/project scope)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `GET`  | `/api/doc-parser/projects/:projectId/documents` | List all documents for a project — id, title, description, file_url, page_count, ocr_table_count, parsed status. Query: `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| `GET`  | `/api/doc-parser/projects/:projectId/documents/:documentId` | Full document detail — metadata + page list with thumbnails + category breakdown + OCR table count |

> **Note:** `/pipeline/page` requires `pdf-lib`. Run `pnpm install` on Windows after any container-side `package.json` change.
#### Pipeline endpoint — quick reference
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"document_id": "<uuid>", "engine": "hybrid"}'
# Returns: { document_id, job_id, pages_persisted, ocr_tables_persisted, summary }
```
> Full docs: `.agents/workflows/parse-document.md`

### Pipeline (Docling engine)
1. **Docling** — extracts text, tables, images from PDF
2. **Classification** — Tesseract + color variance separates floorplans / site photos / diagrams
3. **LLM analysis** — Claude 3.5 Sonnet (via OpenRouter) extracts rooms, doors, windows, areas
4. **Cross-page synthesis** — second LLM call consolidates multi-page results
5. **Confidence scoring** — quality score attached to result

### LLM Provider
Default: `anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet` via OpenRouter (LiteLLM under the hood).
Override with `DEFAULT_LLM_PROVIDER` env var.

---

## Starting the Stack

```powershell
# Full reset + restart (kills zombies, resets DB, seeds, starts dev servers)
powershell -File scripts/reset-and-start.ps1

# Or manually:
npx supabase start
pnpm dev

# Doc-parser (separate):
cd doc-parser && docker compose up -d
```

```powershell
# Kill zombie Node processes on ports 3000-3005
powershell -File scripts/kill-dev-servers.ps1

# Nuclear option
taskkill /F /IM node.exe
```

---

## Supabase Troubleshooting

- **Realtime not updating**: Check RLS policies and that the table is in `supabase_realtime` publication.
- **`supabase start` parse errors**: Comment out `deno_version` / `health_timeout` in `supabase/config.toml`.
- **`major_version`**: Must be `17`. Do not downgrade.

---

## Key Docs

| File | Topic |
|------|-------|
| `docs/ports.md` | Port allocation contract |
| `docs/testing.md` | Test credentials and agent guidelines |
| `docs/understanding_architecture.md` | Architecture deep dive |
| `docs/a2a/README.md` | A2A (Agent-to-Agent) JSON-RPC protocol |
| `docs/development_best_practices.md` | Dev conventions |

# doc-parser Hono API — Complete Route Table (Updated 2026-03-12)

All routes under `/api/doc-parser/*` in `apps/api/src/routes/doc-parser-routes.ts` (1028 lines).
Documentation: `.agents/workflows/parse-document.md` (6 approaches, 293 lines).
AGENTS.md is the single source of truth — sync with `powershell -File scripts/sync-agent-context.ps1`.

## Pipeline endpoints (blocking — submit + poll + persist to DB)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline` | Full pipeline — parse entire PDF, persist all pages + OCR. Blocks until complete. |
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline/page` | Single-page — extract page N via `pdf-lib`, parse, upsert that page only. Body: `{ document_id, page_number, engine? }` |
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline/reprocess` | Idempotent re-run — same as /pipeline but safe to call multiple times (upsert). Returns `reprocessed: true`. |

## Job control endpoints (async — caller manages polling)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/parse` | Submit job, return job_id immediately |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/status/:jobId` | Lightweight poll — safe every 2-5s |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/results/:jobId` | Full results — fetch once after completed |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/health` | Health check including doc-parser reachability |

## Read endpoints (DB queries — no doc-parser call)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/pages` | List all parsed pages. Query: `?category=floorplan|table|site_photo|diagram|mixed|text`, `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/pages/:pageNumber` | Get one page by 1-indexed number (storage_url, thumbnail, category, raw_text, metadata) |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/ocr` | List OCR/table results. Query: `?page_number=N`, `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/ocr/:pageNumber` | OCR for a single page — returns page metadata + all OCR/table rows for that page |

## Document manager endpoints (project/company scope)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/projects/:projectId/documents` | List all documents for a project — id, title, description, file_url, page_count, ocr_table_count, parsed status. Query: `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/projects/:projectId/documents/:documentId` | Full document detail — metadata + page list with thumbnails + category breakdown + OCR table count |

## Schema
- `project_documents.project_id` -> `wo_projects.id` -> `organizations.id`
- `document_pages.document_id` -> `project_documents.id`
- `ocr_results.document_id` -> `project_documents.id`, `ocr_results.page_id` -> `document_pages.id`

## Notes
- `document_id` must exist in `project_documents` table
- Pipeline endpoints use `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` (bypasses RLS)
- `/pipeline/page` requires `pdf-lib` — run `pnpm install` on Windows after package.json change
- DB tables: `document_pages`, `ocr_results` (migration: `20260312000001_document_pages_ocr.sql`)
- Scoping hierarchy: organizations -> wo_projects -> project_documents -> document_pages -> ocr_results

Test artifacts and parsed results should be saved to 'doc-parser/tests/artifacts/runs/' to avoid resource-intensive re-parsing during testing.

The Hono API 'POST /api/doc-parser/pipeline' endpoint automates the full PDF processing workflow: downloading from Supabase, parsing via doc-parser, and persisting results to 'document_pages' and 'ocr_results' tables.

The test PDF 'Sch'eyk DRAWINGS BH 10 08 2024rs_260113_165135.pdf' is located at 'tests/test_seed/' and is symlinked to 'doc-parser/tests/artifacts/pdfs/scheyk_drawings.pdf' for testing purposes.

The 'pdf-lib' library is used in 'apps/api' for single-page PDF extraction. For page-specific operations, use the 'copyPages(srcDoc, indices)' method. Note: 'copyPagesFrom' is an incorrect method name and was corrected in 'doc-parser-routes.ts'.

---
description: Parse Architectural PDFs into structured JSON and persist to Supabase
---
# Document Parser Workflow

This workflow processes architectural PDF documents to extract floorplans, tables, and site photos,
perform AI-based structural analysis, and persist results to Supabase.

Two approaches are available — choose based on your context:

| Approach | Best for | Blocks caller? |
|---|---|---|
| **HTTP Pipeline** (`POST /api/doc-parser/pipeline`) | Other AIs, frontends, automation | Yes (polls internally) |
| **Python CLI** (`parse_document_pipeline.py`) | Local dev, debugging, scripting | Yes |
| **HTTP Proxy** (`POST /api/doc-parser/parse`) | Custom polling loops | No (returns job_id) |

---

## Prerequisites (all approaches)

- Supabase running — `supabase start` on Windows
- doc-parser container running — `docker compose up -d` in `doc-parser/` on Windows
- Migration `20260312000001_document_pages_ocr.sql` applied
- PDF already uploaded to `project-documents` storage bucket and recorded in `project_documents` table

---


## Approach 1 — Full Document Pipeline

Handles the full pipeline internally: submit → poll → persist → return summary. Blocks until complete.

```
POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline
Content-Type: application/json
```

```json
{
  "document_id": "<uuid from project_documents>",
  "engine": "hybrid",
  "poll_interval_ms": 3000,
  "timeout_ms": 600000
}
```

| Field | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `document_id` | Yes | — | UUID from `project_documents` table |
| `engine` | No | `hybrid` | `docling` or `hybrid` |
| `poll_interval_ms` | No | `3000` | How often to poll doc-parser internally |
| `timeout_ms` | No | `600000` | 10 min max wait before 504 |
| `tenant_name` | No | — | Passed to doc-parser for storage namespacing |
| `project_name` | No | `document_id` | Passed to doc-parser |

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"document_id": "<uuid>", "engine": "hybrid"}'
```

**Response:**
```json
{
  "document_id": "<uuid>",
  "job_id": "<doc-parser job id>",
  "engine": "hybrid",
  "status": "completed",
  "pages_persisted": 12,
  "ocr_tables_persisted": 4,
  "summary": { "pages_count": 12, "floorplans_count": 3, "tables_count": 4 },
  "poll_url": "/api/doc-parser/status/<job_id>",
  "results_url": "/api/doc-parser/results/<job_id>"
}
```

---

## Approach 2 — Single-Page Pipeline

Extract page N from the PDF using `pdf-lib`, parse only that page, upsert into `document_pages`.
Useful for re-processing a specific page, previewing, or targeted extraction without re-running the whole document.

> **Requires:** `pdf-lib` — run `pnpm install` on Windows after the `apps/api/package.json` change.

```
POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline/page
Content-Type: application/json
```

```json
{
  "document_id": "<uuid>",
  "page_number": 8,
  "engine": "hybrid",
  "timeout_ms": 120000
}
```

| Field | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `document_id` | Yes | — | UUID from `project_documents` table |
| `page_number` | Yes | — | **1-indexed** page number |
| `engine` | No | `hybrid` | `docling` or `hybrid` |
| `poll_interval_ms` | No | `3000` | Internal poll interval |
| `timeout_ms` | No | `120000` | 2 min default (single page is fast) |

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline/page \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"document_id": "<uuid>", "page_number": 8, "engine": "hybrid"}'
```

**Response:**
```json
{
  "document_id": "<uuid>",
  "job_id": "<doc-parser job id>",
  "engine": "hybrid",
  "page_number": 8,
  "page_id": "<document_pages row uuid>",
  "status": "completed",
  "ocr_tables_persisted": 1,
  "poll_url": "/api/doc-parser/status/<job_id>",
  "results_url": "/api/doc-parser/results/<job_id>"
}
```

---

## Approach 3 — Idempotent Reprocess

Re-run the full pipeline for a document that has already been parsed.
Safe to call multiple times — uses upsert on `document_id,page_number`.
Useful when doc-parser is updated or results need refreshing.

```
POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline/reprocess
Content-Type: application/json
```

Same body as `/pipeline`. Response is identical plus `"reprocessed": true`.

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/pipeline/reprocess \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"document_id": "<uuid>", "engine": "hybrid"}'
```

---

## Approach 4 — Read Endpoints (DB only, no doc-parser call)

Query already-parsed results directly from the database. No doc-parser involved.
Use these to browse results after any pipeline approach has run.

```bash
# List all pages for a document
GET http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/documents/<documentId>/pages

# Filter by category: floorplan | table | site_photo | diagram | mixed | text
GET http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/documents/<documentId>/pages?category=floorplan

# Paginate
GET http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/documents/<documentId>/pages?limit=20&offset=0

# Get a specific page by 1-indexed page number
GET http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/documents/<documentId>/pages/8

# List all OCR/table results
GET http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/documents/<documentId>/ocr

# Filter OCR by page
GET http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/documents/<documentId>/ocr?page_number=8
```

**Pages response:**
```json
{
  "document_id": "<uuid>",
  "pages": [
    {
      "id": "<uuid>",
      "page_number": 1,
      "storage_url": "https://...",
      "thumbnail_url": "https://...",
      "category": "floorplan",
      "raw_text": "...",
      "created_at": "..."
    }
  ],
  "count": 12,
  "offset": 0,
  "limit": 100
}
```

**OCR response:**
```json
{
  "document_id": "<uuid>",
  "ocr_results": [
    {
      "id": "<uuid>",
      "page_id": "<document_pages uuid>",
      "table_index": 0,
      "markdown": "| Col A | Col B |\n|---|---|\n| ... |",
      "structured_data": {},
      "confidence": 0.92
    }
  ],
  "count": 4
}
```

---

## Approach 5 — HTTP Proxy (custom polling)

Returns a `job_id` immediately. Caller is responsible for polling and DB persistence.

```bash
# 1. Submit
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/parse \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"document_id": "<uuid>", "engine": "hybrid"}'
# -> { "job_id": "...", "poll_url": "..." }

# 2. Poll
curl http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/status/<job_id>
# -> { "status": "processing", "progress": { "percent_complete": 45 } }

# 3. Fetch results
curl http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/results/<job_id>
```

---

## Approach 6 — Python CLI Pipeline

Use when running locally or when you need direct control over the pipeline.

### Run
```bash
python doc-parser/parse_document_pipeline.py \
  --pdf path/to/file.pdf \
  --tenant my_tenant \
  --project my_project \
  --supabase-url http://host.docker.internal:54321 \
  --supabase-key <service_role_key>
```

### What it writes to Supabase
| Table | Content |
|---|---|
| `project_documents` | One row — PDF metadata + storage URL |
| `document_pages` | One row per page — category, storage_url, thumbnail_url |
| `document_images` | One row per extracted image (floorplan/site_photo/diagram) |
| `ocr_results` | One row per table — markdown + structured_data JSON |

### Notes
- `server.py` must be rebuilt in Docker after any changes: `docker compose up -d --build`
- Raw results JSON saved alongside PDF as `<name>_pipeline_results.json` for debugging
- `PROGRAM_STATUS=develop_without_ai` in `.env` skips LLM calls (mocked data)

---

## What pipeline endpoints write to Supabase

| Table | Content |
|---|---|
| `document_pages` | One row per page — category, storage_url, thumbnail_url, raw_text, metadata |
| `ocr_results` | One row per extracted table — markdown + structured_data JSON |

> Uses `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` for DB writes (bypasses RLS).
> The PDF must already exist in `project_documents` — upload it first via
> `POST /work-orders/projects/:projectId/documents`.


## Starting the doc-parser container (Windows)

```powershell
cd C:\Users\3900X\Documents\GitHub\agentZero\voicelog-scaffold\doc-parser
docker compose up -d
# or rebuild after server.py changes:
docker compose up -d --build
```

## Health check
```bash
curl http://localhost:5000/health
curl http://localhost:3000/api/doc-parser/health
```

"""Doc-parser FastAPI service.

Integrates all priorities:
- P1: Rich prompts (via prompts.py)
- P2: Improved detection (via detection.py)
- P3: Granular progress tracking (via job_manager.py)
- P4: Persistent jobs, separate status/results endpoints, TTL cleanup
- P5: Cross-page synthesis (via hybrid.py)
- P6: Confidence scoring (via confidence.py)
- P7: Hybrid engine mode (via hybrid.py)

Endpoints:
  POST /parse/docling       - Original Docling-only engine
  POST /parse/hybrid        - Hybrid engine (Docling + Claude)
  GET  /status/{job_id}     - Lightweight status (poll-safe)
  GET  /results/{job_id}    - Full results (fetch-once after completed)
  GET  /health              - Health check
  GET  /jobs                - List all jobs
  DELETE /jobs/{job_id}     - Cancel/delete a job
"""
import os
import re
import json
import uuid
import base64
import asyncio
import logging
import tempfile
import traceback
from typing import Optional
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager

from fastapi import FastAPI, File, UploadFile, Form, BackgroundTasks, HTTPException, Query
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse

from models import (
    JobStatus, ProcessingPhase, EngineType,
    StatusResponse, ResultSummary,
)
from job_manager import (
    JobManager, create_job_manager, PROCESSING_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
)
from prompts import FLOORPLAN_PROMPT_RICH, FLOORPLAN_PROMPT
from detection import (
    is_architectural_drawing, has_architectural_text,
    classify_image, extract_floorplan_candidates, image_to_base64,
)
from confidence import calculate_confidence
from hybrid import process_hybrid, run_llm_extraction, upload_image_to_storage

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.INFO,
    format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(name)s: %(message)s",
)

# ============================================================
# Configuration
# ============================================================

JOB_STORE_BACKEND = os.getenv("JOB_STORE_BACKEND", "memory")  # "memory" or "supabase"
SUPABASE_URL = os.getenv("SUPABASE_URL", "")
SUPABASE_KEY = os.getenv("SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY", "")
DEFAULT_PROVIDER = os.getenv("DEFAULT_LLM_PROVIDER", "anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet")
CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MINUTES = int(os.getenv("CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MINUTES", "10"))

# ============================================================
# Application Lifecycle
# ============================================================

job_manager: Optional[JobManager] = None


@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    """Application startup and shutdown."""
    global job_manager

    # Initialize job manager
    job_manager = create_job_manager(
        backend=JOB_STORE_BACKEND,
        supabase_url=SUPABASE_URL,
        supabase_key=SUPABASE_KEY,
    )

    # Start cleanup loop
    await job_manager.start_cleanup_loop(
        interval_minutes=CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MINUTES
    )

    logger.info(
        f"Doc-parser started (store={JOB_STORE_BACKEND}, "
        f"provider={DEFAULT_PROVIDER})"
    )

    yield

    # Shutdown
    await job_manager.stop_cleanup_loop()
    logger.info("Doc-parser shutting down")


app = FastAPI(
    title="Doc-Parser Service",
    description=(
        "Architectural PDF parsing service using IBM Docling, "
        "Tesseract OCR, and Claude 3.5 Sonnet."
    ),
    version="2.0.0",
    lifespan=lifespan,
)

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=["*"],
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)


# ============================================================
# Helper: Get Supabase client for a specific request
# ============================================================

def get_request_supabase_client(
    supabase_url: Optional[str] = None,
    supabase_key: Optional[str] = None,
):
    """Create a Supabase client from request-level credentials."""
    url = supabase_url or SUPABASE_URL
    key = supabase_key or SUPABASE_KEY
    if not url or not key:
        return None
    try:
        from supabase import create_client
        return create_client(url, key)
    except ImportError as e:
        logger.warning(f"supabase package not installed: {e}")
        return None
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Failed to create Supabase client: {e}")
        return None

# ============================================================
# POST /parse/hybrid - Hybrid Engine (Priority 7)

@app.post("/parse/hybrid")
async def parse_hybrid(
    background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
    file: UploadFile = File(...),
    provider: str = Form(DEFAULT_PROVIDER),
    supabase_url: str = Form(None),
    supabase_key: str = Form(None),
    tenant_name: str = Form(None),
    project_name: str = Form(None),
):
    """Parse PDF using hybrid engine: Docling decomposition + Claude extraction.

    Returns job_id for async polling via /status and /results endpoints.
    """
    temp_path = await save_upload_to_temp(file)
    sb_client = get_request_supabase_client(supabase_url, supabase_key)

    job = await job_manager.create_job(
        file_name=file.filename or "unknown.pdf",
        engine="hybrid",
    )

    background_tasks.add_task(
        _process_hybrid_background,
        job.job_id,
        temp_path,
        provider,
        sb_client,
        tenant_name or "",
        project_name or "",
    )

    return {
        "job_id": job.job_id,
        "status": "queued",
        "engine": "hybrid",
        "file": file.filename,
    }


async def _process_hybrid_background(
    job_id: str,
    temp_path: str,
    provider: str,
    sb_client,
    tenant_name: str,
    project_name: str,
):
    """Background processor for hybrid engine with timeout enforcement."""
    try:
        await asyncio.wait_for(
            process_hybrid(
                job_id=job_id,
                temp_path=temp_path,
                provider=provider,
                job_manager=job_manager,
                sb_client=sb_client,
                tenant_name=tenant_name,
                project_name=project_name,
            ),
            timeout=PROCESSING_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
        )
    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
        await job_manager.timeout_job(job_id)
    except Exception as e:
        traceback.print_exc()
        await job_manager.fail_job(job_id, str(e))


# ============================================================
# GET /status/{job_id} - Lightweight Status (Priority 4a)
# ============================================================

@app.get("/status/{job_id}")
async def get_status(job_id: str):
    """Get lightweight job status for polling.

    NEVER includes result data. Safe to poll every 2-5 seconds.
    Small payload optimized for frequent requests.
    """
    status = await job_manager.get_status(job_id)
    if not status:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Job {job_id} not found")
    return status.model_dump()


# ============================================================
# GET /results/{job_id} - Full Results (Priority 4a)
# ============================================================

@app.get("/results/{job_id}")
async def get_results(
    job_id: str,
    summary: bool = Query(False, description="Return lightweight summary only"),
):
    """Get full extraction results for a completed job.

    Heavy payload - fetch once after status confirms 'completed'.
    Use ?summary=true for a lightweight preview.
    """
    # Check job exists and is completed
    status = await job_manager.get_status(job_id)
    if not status:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Job {job_id} not found")

    if status.status == JobStatus.PROCESSING or status.status == JobStatus.QUEUED:
        return {
            "job_id": job_id,
            "status": status.status.value,
            "message": "Job is still processing. Poll /status/{job_id} for progress.",
        }

    if status.status in (JobStatus.FAILED, JobStatus.TIMEOUT):
        return {
            "job_id": job_id,
            "status": status.status.value,
            "error": status.error,
        }

    # Return summary or full results
    if summary:
        result_summary = await job_manager.get_result_summary(job_id)
        if not result_summary:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Results not found")
        return {
            "job_id": job_id,
            "status": "completed",
            "summary": result_summary.model_dump(),
        }

    result = await job_manager.get_result(job_id)
    if not result:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Results not found")

    return {
        "job_id": job_id,
        "status": "completed",
        "result": result,
    }


# ============================================================
# GET /health - Health Check
# ============================================================

@app.get("/health")
async def health_check():
    """Service health check."""
    # Check Docling availability
    docling_available = False
    try:
        import docling
        docling_available = True
    except ImportError:
        pass

    # Check Tesseract availability
    tesseract_available = False
    try:
        import pytesseract
        pytesseract.get_tesseract_version()
        tesseract_available = True
    except Exception:
        pass

    # Check LiteLLM availability
    litellm_available = False
    try:
        import litellm
        litellm_available = True
    except ImportError:
        pass

    jobs = await job_manager.list_jobs()
    running_count = sum(1 for j in jobs if j.status == JobStatus.PROCESSING)
    queued_count = sum(1 for j in jobs if j.status == JobStatus.QUEUED)

    return {
        "status": "healthy",
        "version": "2.0.0",
        "engines": ["docling", "hybrid"],
        "dependencies": {
            "docling": docling_available,
            "tesseract": tesseract_available,
            "litellm": litellm_available,
        },
        "jobs": {
            "running": running_count,
            "queued": queued_count,
            "total": len(jobs),
        },
        "config": {
            "store_backend": JOB_STORE_BACKEND,
            "default_provider": DEFAULT_PROVIDER,
            "processing_timeout_s": PROCESSING_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
        },
    }


# ============================================================
# GET /jobs - List Jobs
# ============================================================

@app.get("/jobs")
async def list_jobs(
    status: Optional[str] = Query(None, description="Filter by status"),
):
    """List all jobs with optional status filter."""
    jobs = await job_manager.list_jobs(status)
    return {
        "jobs": [
            {
                "job_id": j.job_id,
                "status": j.status.value,
                "engine": j.engine if isinstance(j.engine, str) else j.engine.value,
                "file_name": j.file_name,
                "progress": j.progress.model_dump(),
                "created_at": j.created_at.isoformat(),
                "updated_at": j.updated_at.isoformat(),
            }
            for j in jobs
        ],
        "total": len(jobs),
    }


# ============================================================
# DELETE /jobs/{job_id} - Delete Job
# ============================================================

@app.delete("/jobs/{job_id}")
async def delete_job(job_id: str):
    """Delete a job and its results."""
    job = await job_manager._store.get_job(job_id)
    if not job:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Job {job_id} not found")

    if job.status == JobStatus.PROCESSING:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=409,
            detail="Cannot delete a running job. Wait for completion or timeout.",
        )

    # Delete result and job record
    await job_manager._store.delete_expired()  # Piggyback cleanup
    return {"deleted": job_id}


# ============================================================
# Legacy Compatibility: GET /result/{job_id}
# Redirects to new /results endpoint
# ============================================================

@app.get("/result/{job_id}")
async def get_result_legacy(job_id: str):
    """Legacy endpoint - redirects to /results/{job_id}."""
    return await get_results(job_id, summary=False)


# ============================================================
# Entry Point
# ============================================================

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import uvicorn
    uvicorn.run(
        "server:app",
        host="0.0.0.0",
        port=int(os.getenv("PORT", "5000")),
        reload=os.getenv("ENV", "production") == "development",
    )

kvps.memories:
# doc-parser Hono API — Complete Route Table (Updated 2026-03-12)

All routes under `/api/doc-parser/*` in `apps/api/src/routes/doc-parser-routes.ts` (1028 lines).
Documentation: `.agents/workflows/parse-document.md` (6 approaches, 293 lines).
AGENTS.md is the single source of truth — sync with `powershell -File scripts/sync-agent-context.ps1`.

## Pipeline endpoints (blocking — submit + poll + persist to DB)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline` | Full pipeline — parse entire PDF, persist all pages + OCR. Blocks until complete. |
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline/page` | Single-page — extract page N via `pdf-lib`, parse, upsert that page only. Body: `{ document_id, page_number, engine? }` |
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline/reprocess` | Idempotent re-run — same as /pipeline but safe to call multiple times (upsert). Returns `reprocessed: true`. |

## Job control endpoints (async — caller manages polling)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/parse` | Submit job, return job_id immediately |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/status/:jobId` | Lightweight poll — safe every 2-5s |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/results/:jobId` | Full results — fetch once after completed |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/health` | Health check including doc-parser reachability |

## Read endpoints (DB queries — no doc-parser call)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/pages` | List all parsed pages. Query: `?category=floorplan|table|site_photo|diagram|mixed|text`, `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/pages/:pageNumber` | Get one page by 1-indexed number (storage_url, thumbnail, category, raw_text, metadata) |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/ocr` | List OCR/table results. Query: `?page_number=N`, `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/ocr/:pageNumber` | OCR for a single page — returns page metadata + all OCR/table rows for that page |

## Document manager endpoints (project/company scope)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/projects/:projectId/documents` | List all documents for a project — id, title, description, file_url, page_count, ocr_table_count, parsed status. Query: `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/projects/:projectId/documents/:documentId` | Full document detail — metadata + page list with thumbnails + category breakdown + OCR table count |

## Schema
- `project_documents.project_id` -> `wo_projects.id` -> `organizations.id`
- `document_pages.document_id` -> `project_documents.id`
- `ocr_results.document_id` -> `project_documents.id`, `ocr_results.page_id` -> `document_pages.id`

## Notes
- `document_id` must exist in `project_documents` table
- Pipeline endpoints use `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` (bypasses RLS)
- `/pipeline/page` requires `pdf-lib` — run `pnpm install` on Windows after package.json change
- DB tables: `document_pages`, `ocr_results` (migration: `20260312000001_document_pages_ocr.sql`)
- Scoping hierarchy: organizations -> wo_projects -> project_documents -> document_pages -> ocr_results

Test artifacts and parsed results should be saved to 'doc-parser/tests/artifacts/runs/' to avoid resource-intensive re-parsing during testing.

The Hono API 'POST /api/doc-parser/pipeline' endpoint automates the full PDF processing workflow: downloading from Supabase, parsing via doc-parser, and persisting results to 'document_pages' and 'ocr_results' tables.

The test PDF 'Sch'eyk DRAWINGS BH 10 08 2024rs_260113_165135.pdf' is located at 'tests/test_seed/' and is symlinked to 'doc-parser/tests/artifacts/pdfs/scheyk_drawings.pdf' for testing purposes.

The 'pdf-lib' library is used in 'apps/api' for single-page PDF extraction. For page-specific operations, use the 'copyPages(srcDoc, indices)' method. Note: 'copyPagesFrom' is an incorrect method name and was corrected in 'doc-parser-routes.ts'.

# ============================================================
# Output Assembly
# ============================================================

def assemble_hybrid_output(
    job_id: str,
    unit_extractions: List[Dict[str, Any]],
    table_urls: List[Dict[str, Any]],
    thumbnail_urls: Dict[int, str],
    floorplan_dia_urls: Dict[int, List[str]],
    floorplan_urls: Dict[int, List[str]],
    photo_urls: Dict[int, List[str]],
    diagram_urls: Dict[int, List[str]],
    raw_llm_responses: Dict[str, str],
    total_pages: int,
    page_elements: Dict[int, List[Dict[str, Any]]],
    footer_sample: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """Assemble final output from all pipeline phases."""

    # Build per-page data
    pages: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
    for page_no in range(1, total_pages + 1):
        page_key = str(page_no)
        page_data: Dict[str, Any] = {
            "thumbnail_url": thumbnail_urls.get(page_no, ""),
            "floorplan_dia_urls": floorplan_dia_urls.get(page_no, []),
            "floorplan_urls": floorplan_urls.get(page_no, []),
            "site_photos": [],
            "diagrams": [],
            "tables": [],
            "elements_detected": page_elements.get(page_no, []),
        }

        # Collect photos for this page
        if page_no in photo_urls:
            page_data["site_photos"].extend(photo_urls[page_no])

        # Collect diagrams for this page
        if page_no in diagram_urls:
            page_data["diagrams"].extend(diagram_urls[page_no])

        # Collect tables for this page
        for t in table_urls:
            if t.get("page_no") == page_no:
                page_data["tables"].append({
                    "markdown": t.get("markdown", ""),
                    "url": t.get("url", ""),
                    "image_url": t.get("image_url", ""),
                    "source": t.get("source", "unknown"),
                })

        pages[page_key] = page_data

    # Build units list
    units = []
    for u in unit_extractions:
        unit = dict(u)
        # Ensure image_url is set
        if not unit.get("image_url"):
            sp = unit.get("source_page")
            if sp and sp in floorplan_urls:
                urls_for_page = floorplan_urls[sp]
                unit["image_url"] = urls_for_page[0] if urls_for_page else ""
        units.append(unit)

    # Collect all photo URLs across pages
    all_photos = []
    for urls in photo_urls.values():
        all_photos.extend(urls)

    all_diagrams = []
    for urls in diagram_urls.values():
        all_diagrams.extend(urls)

    # Summary
    total_floorplans = sum(len(v) for v in floorplan_urls.values())
    total_floorplan_dias = sum(len(v) for v in floorplan_dia_urls.values())
    summary = {
        "total_pages": total_pages,
        "units_found": len(units),
        "floorplans_found": total_floorplans,
        "floorplan_dias_found": total_floorplan_dias,
        "tables_found": len(table_urls),
        "photos_found": len(all_photos),
        "diagrams_found": len(all_diagrams),
        "thumbnails_count": len(thumbnail_urls),
        "pages_with_elements": len(page_elements),
    }

    # Confidence
    try:
        confidence = calculate_confidence(
            units, table_urls,
            total_pages=total_pages,
            floorplans_found=total_floorplans,
        )
    except Exception:
        confidence = {"overall": HYBRID_PARAMS.FALLBACK_OVERALL_CONFIDENCE, "note": "confidence calculation failed"}

    return {
        "job_id": job_id,
        "engine": "hybrid_page_render",
        "timestamp": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
        "summary": summary,
        "confidence": confidence,
        "units": units,
        "pages": pages,
        "tables": table_urls,
        "footer": footer_sample,
        "thumbnails": dict(thumbnail_urls),
        "raw_llm_responses": {str(k): v for k, v in raw_llm_responses.items()},
    }

        "  pages_result[page_no]['thumbnail_url'] = thumb_url  (line ~426)",
        "",
        "So the bug has TWO dimensions:",
        "1. Hono fallback chain doesn't include 'page_image_url' → misses docling engine output",
        "2. Hybrid engine doesn't output page_image_url at all → no full page render URL",
        "",
        "But the user's task says: 'Do NOT change server.py — other consumers may depend on page_image_url'",
        "And the fix approach is: update the Hono fallback chain only",
        "",
        "For the Hono fix, I need to update TWO locations in doc-parser-routes.ts:",
        "1. executePipeline() around line 181: storage_url: pd?.storage_url ?? pd?.page_image_url ?? pd?.image_url ?? null",
        "2. pipeline/page route around line 562: same fix",
        "",
        "Now let me respond to the user with my analysis and ask for the prerequisites before starting"
    ],
    "headline": "Presenting analysis and session plan to user",
    "tool_name": "response",
    "tool_args": {
        "text": "# Session Analysis Complete ✅

I've read all 4 files and analyzed the codebase. Here's my assessment:

---

## 🐛 Bug Analysis — Image URL Mismatch

**Root cause confirmed across both engines:**

| Engine | Field set for full page render | Hono looks for |
|--------|-------------------------------|----------------|
| **Docling** (`server.py` ~line 418) | `page_image_url` | `storage_url` → `image_url` ❌ miss |
| **Hybrid** (`hybrid.py` ~line 1569) | *(not set at all!)* | `storage_url` → `image_url` ❌ miss |

```typescript

kvps.memories:
Doc-parser must run as a standalone Docker container on port 5000; it cannot be run or modified (pip/venv) directly from the Agent Zero container.

Doc-parser & Hono API Architecture (Windows Host/Docker):

1. Infrastructure & Orchestration:
- Hono API ('The Brain'): Runs on port 3000. Acts as the primary orchestration hub and proxy.
- Doc-parser Service: Runs in a dedicated Docker container on port 5000 (FastAPI-based).
- Network Context: Both services run in separate Docker containers on the Windows host. Access via 'host.docker.internal'.
- CRITICAL RULE: Doc-parser must run as a standalone Docker container; it cannot be run or modified (pip/venv) directly from the Agent Zero container. Never attempt to run 'python server.py' locally. Use `docker compose up` on the host.

2. Processing Pipeline (5-Phase Workflow):
- Phase 1: Upload to Supabase Storage ('project-documents' bucket, 50MB limit).
- Phase 2: Async parsing via Doc-parser (Port 5000). Single-page tasks use `pdf-lib` within Hono to isolate pages.
- Phase 3: Metadata recording in `document_pages` table.
- Phase 4: Element categorization/extraction via LLM/CV2.
- Phase 5: OCR persistence in `ocr_results` table.
- KNOWN BUG & SOURCE: Full-page images fail to persist because the doc-parser engine (server.py lines 418/426) returns 'page_image_url' and 'thumbnail_url', while the Hono API (doc-parser-routes.ts lines 181 and 562) expects 'storage_url' or 'image_url', resulting in NULL values in the database.

3. API Endpoints (Hono Port 3000):
- Implementation: 17 endpoints under `/api/doc-parser/*` covering pipeline, extraction, and management.
- Key Routes: `/api/doc-parser/pipeline` (Automates full workflow), `/pipeline/page` (Single), `/pipeline/reprocess` (Idempotent), `/parse` (Proxy/Polling).

4. Data & Storage:
- Hierarchy: organizations -> wo_projects -> project_documents -> document_pages -> ocr_results.
- Storage: Supabase buckets with local fallback at `parsed_images/` and `parsed_tables/`.
- Testing: Save test artifacts to 'doc-parser/tests/artifacts/runs/'.

# doc-parser Hono API — Complete Route Table (Updated 2026-03-12)

All routes under `/api/doc-parser/*` in `apps/api/src/routes/doc-parser-routes.ts` (1028 lines).
Documentation: `.agents/workflows/parse-document.md` (6 approaches, 293 lines).
AGENTS.md is the single source of truth — sync with `powershell -File scripts/sync-agent-context.ps1`.

## Pipeline endpoints (blocking — submit + poll + persist to DB)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline` | Full pipeline — parse entire PDF, persist all pages + OCR. Blocks until complete. |
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline/page` | Single-page — extract page N via `pdf-lib`, parse, upsert that page only. Body: `{ document_id, page_number, engine? }` |
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline/reprocess` | Idempotent re-run — same as /pipeline but safe to call multiple times (upsert). Returns `reprocessed: true`. |

## Job control endpoints (async — caller manages polling)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/parse` | Submit job, return job_id immediately |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/status/:jobId` | Lightweight poll — safe every 2-5s |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/results/:jobId` | Full results — fetch once after completed |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/health` | Health check including doc-parser reachability |

## Read endpoints (DB queries — no doc-parser call)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/pages` | List all parsed pages. Query: `?category=floorplan|table|site_photo|diagram|mixed|text`, `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/pages/:pageNumber` | Get one page by 1-indexed number (storage_url, thumbnail, category, raw_text, metadata) |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/ocr` | List OCR/table results. Query: `?page_number=N`, `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/ocr/:pageNumber` | OCR for a single page — returns page metadata + all OCR/table rows for that page |

## Document manager endpoints (project/company scope)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/projects/:projectId/documents` | List all documents for a project — id, title, description, file_url, page_count, ocr_table_count, parsed status. Query: `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/projects/:projectId/documents/:documentId` | Full document detail — metadata + page list with thumbnails + category breakdown + OCR table count |

## Schema
- `project_documents.project_id` -> `wo_projects.id` -> `organizations.id`
- `document_pages.document_id` -> `project_documents.id`
- `ocr_results.document_id` -> `project_documents.id`, `ocr_results.page_id` -> `document_pages.id`

## Notes
- `document_id` must exist in `project_documents` table
- Pipeline endpoints use `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` (bypasses RLS)
- `/pipeline/page` requires `pdf-lib` — run `pnpm install` on Windows after package.json change
- DB tables: `document_pages`, `ocr_results` (migration: `20260312000001_document_pages_ocr.sql`)
- Scoping hierarchy: organizations -> wo_projects -> project_documents -> document_pages -> ocr_results

Parsed artifacts (PNG, TXT, JSON, MD) should be saved to 'doc-parser/tests/artifacts/runs/' with a 'manifest.json' and 'full_result.json' to avoid resource-intensive re-parsing during testing.
kvps.solutions:
# Problem
 A field name mismatch between the `doc-parser` service and the Hono API pipeline causes full-page image URLs to be lost (persisted as NULL).
# Solution
 1. In the Hono API route (`doc-parser-routes.ts`), update the mapping logic to include the specific field returned by the service.
2. Change the assignment from `storage_url: pd?.storage_url ?? pd?.image_url ?? null` to include `page_image_url`.
3. Correct mapping: `storage_url: pd?.storage_url ?? pd?.page_image_url ?? pd?.image_url ?? null`.

Doc-parser & Hono API Architecture (Windows Host/Docker):

1. Infrastructure & Orchestration:
- Hono API ('The Brain'): Runs on port 3000. Acts as the primary orchestration hub and proxy.
- Doc-parser Service: Runs in a dedicated Docker container on port 5000 (FastAPI-based).
- Network Context: Both services run in separate Docker containers on the Windows host. Access via 'host.docker.internal'.
- CRITICAL RULE: Doc-parser must run as a standalone Docker container; it cannot be run or modified (pip/venv) directly from the Agent Zero container. Never attempt to run 'python server.py' locally. Use `docker compose up` on the host.

2. Processing Pipeline (5-Phase Workflow):
- Phase 1: Upload to Supabase Storage ('project-documents' bucket, 50MB limit).
- Phase 2: Async parsing via Doc-parser (Port 5000). Single-page tasks use `pdf-lib` within Hono to isolate pages.
- Phase 3: Metadata recording in `document_pages` table.
- Phase 4: Element categorization/extraction via LLM/CV2.
- Phase 5: OCR persistence in `ocr_results` table.
- KNOWN BUG & SOURCE: Full-page images fail to persist because the doc-parser engine (server.py lines 418/426) returns 'page_image_url' and 'thumbnail_url', while the Hono API (doc-parser-routes.ts lines 181 and 562) expects 'storage_url' or 'image_url', resulting in NULL values in the database.

3. API Endpoints (Hono Port 3000):
- Implementation: 17 endpoints under `/api/doc-parser/*` covering pipeline, extraction, and management.
- Key Routes: `/api/doc-parser/pipeline` (Automates full workflow), `/pipeline/page` (Single), `/pipeline/reprocess` (Idempotent), `/parse` (Proxy/Polling).

4. Data & Storage:
- Hierarchy: organizations -> wo_projects -> project_documents -> document_pages -> ocr_results.
- Storage: Supabase buckets with local fallback at `parsed_images/` and `parsed_tables/`.
- Testing: Save test artifacts to 'doc-parser/tests/artifacts/runs/'.

# doc-parser Hono API — Complete Route Table (Updated 2026-03-12)

All routes under `/api/doc-parser/*` in `apps/api/src/routes/doc-parser-routes.ts` (1028 lines).
Documentation: `.agents/workflows/parse-document.md` (6 approaches, 293 lines).
AGENTS.md is the single source of truth — sync with `powershell -File scripts/sync-agent-context.ps1`.

## Pipeline endpoints (blocking — submit + poll + persist to DB)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline` | Full pipeline — parse entire PDF, persist all pages + OCR. Blocks until complete. |
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline/page` | Single-page — extract page N via `pdf-lib`, parse, upsert that page only. Body: `{ document_id, page_number, engine? }` |
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/pipeline/reprocess` | Idempotent re-run — same as /pipeline but safe to call multiple times (upsert). Returns `reprocessed: true`. |

## Job control endpoints (async — caller manages polling)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/doc-parser/parse` | Submit job, return job_id immediately |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/status/:jobId` | Lightweight poll — safe every 2-5s |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/results/:jobId` | Full results — fetch once after completed |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/health` | Health check including doc-parser reachability |

## Read endpoints (DB queries — no doc-parser call)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/pages` | List all parsed pages. Query: `?category=floorplan|table|site_photo|diagram|mixed|text`, `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/pages/:pageNumber` | Get one page by 1-indexed number (storage_url, thumbnail, category, raw_text, metadata) |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/ocr` | List OCR/table results. Query: `?page_number=N`, `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/documents/:documentId/ocr/:pageNumber` | OCR for a single page — returns page metadata + all OCR/table rows for that page |

## Document manager endpoints (project/company scope)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/projects/:projectId/documents` | List all documents for a project — id, title, description, file_url, page_count, ocr_table_count, parsed status. Query: `?limit=`, `?offset=` |
| GET | `/api/doc-parser/projects/:projectId/documents/:documentId` | Full document detail — metadata + page list with thumbnails + category breakdown + OCR table count |

## Schema
- `project_documents.project_id` -> `wo_projects.id` -> `organizations.id`
- `document_pages.document_id` -> `project_documents.id`
- `ocr_results.document_id` -> `project_documents.id`, `ocr_results.page_id` -> `document_pages.id`

## Notes
- `document_id` must exist in `project_documents` table
- Pipeline endpoints use `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` (bypasses RLS)
- `/pipeline/page` requires `pdf-lib` — run `pnpm install` on Windows after package.json change
- DB tables: `document_pages`, `ocr_results` (migration: `20260312000001_document_pages_ocr.sql`)
- Scoping hierarchy: organizations -> wo_projects -> project_documents -> document_pages -> ocr_results
